creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
Matthias Andree <mandree@FreeBSD.org> writes:

> I fail to see the problem, only that this would undermine the documented purpose
> of make.conf, namely affecting the build of the application (as opposed to the
> result).

That's some new meaning you've got there. Things like setting CC/CXX,
CFLAGS, using sudo(1) in SU_CMD, using curl/wget in FETCH_CMD, forcing
make(1) jobs, building with debug symbols or overriding default python
version are exactly what make.conf is for. It's even described in
Porter's Handbook and Mk/bsd.port.mk.

I for one want to utilize bash emulation in zsh. Why it should be
restricted? I know exactly what I'm doing.

>
> xmlto requires bash and uses the value from BASH_CMD, so we don't want to
> override anything here, particularly not to zsh.

Even if it does work with zsh without producing errors?

>
> What problem are you trying to solve?

Giving user a choice via well defined place, namely make.conf. I hope
you're not assuming that user is not responsible for contents of said
file.
